hero.title:
Nuxt App
Template
hero.description: A Nuxt 4 template that keeps framework conventions while demonstrating file routing, i18n, Pinia state, ofetch API layering, and MSW mock integration.

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.description: Use Nuxt-native commands for development, SSR build, SSG output, and quality checks.

dev.title: Development
dev.description: Start the Nuxt dev server with HMR.

build.title: SSR Build
build.description: Build the production Nitro server output.

generate.title: Static Output
generate.description: Generate static pages when the project targets SSG.

lint.title: Quality Gate
lint.description: Run lint checks before shipping changes.

features.title: Core Capabilities
features.description: Show the Nuxt-specific capabilities this template should teach first.

items.routing: Pages stay in src/pages so routing remains framework-native and predictable.
items.i18n: Language files use BCP 47 names and Nuxt i18n handles localized routes.
items.pinia: Runtime interaction state lives in src/stores/demo with explicit actions.
items.api: src/api/request.ts wraps ofetch while business calls stay in src/api/demo.ts.
items.mock: The shared mock package can intercept /api/template-demo/* during local development.
items.modules: Pinia, i18n, and Element Plus are registered as Nuxt modules, not app.use calls.

structure.title: Directory Structure
structure.description: Nuxt keeps its framework directories; modularity is expressed through pages, components, stores, and api boundaries.

components.responsibility: High-cohesion demo sections with internal display data.

state.responsibility: Pinia runtime state, actions, and interaction flow.

api.responsibility: ofetch adapter, shared response types, and business API calls.

runtime.title: Runtime Demo
runtime.description: Small interactions showing Nuxt i18n, Pinia state, and API/mock boundaries.

i18n.heading: Localized Nuxt Runtime
i18n.description: Switching language goes through Nuxt i18n and updates the localized route context.
